title: Chittagong Diabetes Data Analysis
text:
Overview
This project documents a reproducible workflow for preparing and analyzing diabetes patient data from the Chittagong region. The current version of the project focuses on two major stages: cleaning and standardizing the raw clinical dataset, then analyzing the cleaned records by Thana, City Corporation classification, and age group.
The analysis pipeline is designed to support transparent reporting and future extension. Dataset paths are managed through environment variables, cleaned records are exported for downstream analysis, and location-based summaries are generated from the standardized dataset.
crumbs: 1  Chittagong Diabetes Data Analysis

title: Chittagong Diabetes Data Analysis
text:
Project Scope
The project currently covers the following analytical areas:

Cleaning raw diabetes patient records and removing duplicate entries.
Updating fasting glucose values from a supplementary dataset using patient ID.
Standardizing mixed-format clinical variables into analysis-ready numeric fields.
Removing records with missing key analytical fields such as Fasting, Age, and Thana where required.
Standardizing Thana names to reduce spelling and administrative-label inconsistencies.
Separating records into City Corporation and outside City Corporation groups.
Creating age groups using the threshold <=30 and >30.
Calculating population counts, Thana-wise distributions, and percentage breakdowns.
Verifying whether the final regional grouping accounts for the full filtered dataset.
crumbs: 1  Chittagong Diabetes Data Analysis

title: Chittagong Diabetes Data Analysis
text:
Analysis Workflow
The project is organized into a sequential workflow. Each stage produces outputs that support the next stage of analysis.

Data Cleaning and Standardization
The first stage prepares the raw diabetes dataset for analysis. It loads the primary dataset and supplementary fasting-value dataset from environment-defined paths, removes duplicate columns and duplicate patient records, updates fasting values, standardizes numeric fields, and exports the cleaned dataset.
Key tasks in this stage include:

Loading the raw Excel dataset from DATASET_PATH.
Removing duplicate columns.
Deduplicating records by ID, keeping the last available record.
Loading the supplementary file from NEW_PATH.
Updating the Fasting column by matching records on ID.
Converting BMI, Weight (KG), and Height (CM) to numeric values.
Extracting numeric values from mixed-format Fasting entries.
Removing records with missing fasting glucose or age values.
Saving the cleaned dataset to CLEANED_DATASET_PATH.

The complete documentation for this stage is available in:
data_cleaning_and_standardization.qmd


Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis
The second stage uses the cleaned dataset to examine the regional distribution of patient records. It focuses on Thana-level classification, City Corporation grouping, age-group segmentation, and percentage-based summaries.
Key tasks in this stage include:

Loading the cleaned dataset from CLEANED_DATASET_PATH.
Removing Thanas outside the selected analysis scope.
Dropping records with missing Thana values.
Mapping alternative Thana names to standardized labels.
Defining Thanas inside and outside the City Corporation.
Creating the Age Group variable using <=30 and >30 categories.
Calculating total population records after filtering.
Summarizing records inside the City Corporation.
Summarizing records outside the City Corporation.
Calculating Thana-level and age-group percentages.
Running a final verification check to identify unmapped or unclassified records.

The complete documentation for this stage is available in:
thana_population_distribution_analysis.qmd

Current Dataset Preparation Logic
The latest data preparation workflow uses the following assumptions and rules:

ID is treated as the unique patient identifier for deduplication.
If multiple records share the same ID, the last occurrence is retained.
The supplementary dataset is used to update fasting glucose values in the primary dataset.
Fasting glucose values may contain units or mixed text, so the first numeric value is extracted.
Records without valid fasting glucose values are removed from the cleaned dataset.
Records without age values are removed from the cleaned dataset.
Thana names are standardized before regional analysis.
Records without valid Thana values are removed before Thana-level analysis.

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.1 Overview
This document describes a reproducible data cleaning and standardization workflow for a diabetes patient dataset. The purpose of this workflow is to transform raw patient records into a cleaner and more analysis-ready dataset by removing duplicate fields and records, updating fasting glucose values from a supplementary file, standardizing mixed-format numeric columns, and exporting the cleaned dataset for downstream analysis.
The workflow relies on environment variables to locate the raw dataset, supplementary update file, and final cleaned output path. This approach avoids hard-coding local file locations and makes the pipeline easier to reuse across different environments.
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.2 Objectives
The main objectives of this cleaning process are to:

Load the raw diabetes dataset from a configured environment path.
Inspect the structure and column inventory of the dataset.
Remove duplicate columns created during data collection or export.
Remove duplicate patient records using ID as the unique identifier.
Update the primary dataset with corrected or newly available fasting glucose values from a supplementary dataset.
Convert selected anthropometric variables to numeric format.
Extract numeric fasting glucose values from mixed text formats.
Remove records that are missing key analytical fields such as Fasting and Age.
Export the cleaned dataset to the configured output path.
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.3 Expected Input and Output
This workflow expects the following environment variables to be available:

DATASET_PATH: path to the primary raw Excel dataset.
NEW_PATH: path to the supplementary Excel dataset containing updated fasting glucose values.
CLEANED_DATASET_PATH: path where the cleaned Excel dataset will be saved.

The expected output is a cleaned Excel dataset containing standardized patient records suitable for subsequent analysis, modeling, or reporting.

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.8 Remove Duplicate Columns
Duplicate columns are removed from the dataset. This can happen when Excel files are exported, merged, or manually edited. Keeping only the first occurrence of each column name prevents ambiguity during later transformations.

df = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.duplicated()]
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.9 Remove Duplicate Records
Duplicate patient records are removed using ID as the unique patient identifier. When multiple records share the same ID, the last occurrence is retained. This assumes that later entries are more complete or more recently updated.

df.drop_duplicates(subset="ID", keep="last", inplace=True)
df.__len__()

44136
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
section: 2.10 Update the Original Dataset with Cleaned Data
text:
2.10 Update the Original Dataset with Cleaned Data
A supplementary dataset is loaded from the path stored in NEW_PATH. This file is used to update the Fasting column in the primary dataset.
Before merging, duplicate records are also removed from the supplementary dataset using the same ID-based rule. Copies of the ID and Fasting columns are created for comparison and verification purposes before the update is applied.

new_file = os.getenv("NEW_PATH")
new_df = pd.read_excel(new_file)
new_df.drop_duplicates(subset="ID", keep="last", inplace=True)

# Create index copies for comparison before merge
df_before_fasting = df[["ID", "Fasting"]].copy()
new_df_fasting = new_df[["ID", "Fasting"]].copy()

The update is performed by temporarily setting ID as the index in both datasets. The Fasting values in the primary dataset are then updated using matching ID values from the supplementary dataset. After the update, the index is reset so that ID returns to a regular column.

# Perform merge using ID-based index
df.set_index("ID", inplace=True)
new_df.set_index("ID", inplace=True)
df.update(new_df["Fasting"])
df.reset_index(inplace=True)

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
section: 2.15 Convert Anthropometric Columns to Numeric Format
text:
2.15 Convert Anthropometric Columns to Numeric Format
The columns BMI, Weight (KG), and Height (CM) are converted to numeric data types. Any non-convertible values are coerced to missing values using errors="coerce".
This step ensures that these fields can be used reliably in later statistical analysis, visualization, or machine learning workflows.

cols = ["BMI", "Weight (KG)", "Height (CM)"]

for col in cols:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce")
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
section: 2.16 Standardize Fasting Column to Numerical Values
text:
2.16 Standardize Fasting Column to Numerical Values
The original Fasting column may contain mixed-format values, such as numbers combined with units or text. A regular expression is used to extract the first numeric value from each entry.
For example, a value such as 10.0MMOL is converted to 10.0. Records without a valid numeric fasting value are treated as missing.

import re   
def extract_numerical_value(value):
    if pd.isna(value):
        return None
    match = re.search(r"(\d+\.?\d*)", str(value))
    return float(match.group(1)) if match else None
df["Fasting"] = df["Fasting"].apply(extract_numerical_value)
df["Fasting"] = df["Fasting"].astype(float)
df.dropna(subset=["Fasting"], inplace=True)
crumbs: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization

This verification step confirms how many records have valid numerical fasting values after extraction and type conversion. It also reports the number of missing fasting values and the remaining dataset size.

print(f"Records with missing Fasting: {df['Fasting'].isna().sum()}")
print(f"\nTotal records: {len(df)}")

title: 2  Data Cleaning and Standardization
text:
2.20 Cleaning Summary
This cleaning workflow performs the following transformations:

Loads the primary diabetes patient dataset from an environment-defined path.
Removes duplicate columns from the raw dataset.
Removes duplicate patient records using ID, keeping the last occurrence.
Loads a supplementary dataset and updates the primary Fasting values by matching on ID.
Verifies the fasting-value update through count summaries and sample inspection.
Converts BMI, Weight (KG), and Height (CM) to numeric format.
Extracts numeric values from mixed-format fasting glucose entries.
Removes records missing valid fasting glucose values.
Removes records missing age values.
Saves the cleaned dataset for downstream analysis.

title: 4  Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis
text:
4.1 Overview
This document presents a reproducible analysis workflow for examining population records by Thana, City Corporation classification, and Age Group. The experiment focuses on cleaning location labels, separating records into areas inside and outside the City Corporation, summarizing population counts, and calculating percentage distributions.
The analysis uses a cleaned Excel dataset loaded from an environment variable. The workflow is designed to support transparent reporting, verification, and future replication without hard-coding the input file path.
crumbs: 4  Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis

title: 4  Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis
text:
4.2 Objectives
The main objectives of this experiment are to:

Load the cleaned dataset from a configured environment path.
Remove records from Thanas that are outside the intended analysis scope.
Standardize inconsistent or alternative Thana names through mapping.
Classify Thanas into City Corporation and non-City Corporation groups.
Create an age-based grouping using a threshold of 30 years.
Produce total population counts and Thana-wise summaries.
Calculate percentage distributions for City Corporation, outside City Corporation, and overall dataset shares.
Run a verification check to confirm whether all records are accounted for in the final grouping.

section: 4.5 Initial Data Loading and Scope Filtering
text:
4.5 Initial Data Loading and Scope Filtering
The cleaned dataset is loaded from the Excel file specified in the environment variable. A predefined list of Thanas is then excluded from the analysis. These excluded values represent locations that are not part of the intended analytical scope for this experiment.
Records with missing Thana values are also removed because the location field is essential for all subsequent grouping, classification, and percentage calculations.

path = os.getenv("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH")
if path is None:
    raise ValueError("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH not set")

df = pd.read_excel(path)

values = ["Senbag", "Shahjadpur", "Barkal", "Chandina", "Chatkhil",
          "Chouddagram", "Companyganj", "Cox's Bazar Sadar",
          "Cumilla Adarsha Sadar", "Jorargonj", "Kawkhali",
          "Khagrachhari Sadar", "Kobirhat", "Manikchhari",
          "Matiranga", "Moheshkhali", "Muradnagar", "Nabinagar",
          "Noakhali Sadar", "Ramgarh", "Rangamati Sadar",
          "Shahrasti", "Sonagazi", "Ukhia", "Hatia", "Chakaria"]

df = df.loc[~df['Thana'].isin(values)]

df.dropna(subset=["Thana"], inplace=True)

section: 4.6 City Corporation and Outside City Corporation Classification
text:
4.6 City Corporation and Outside City Corporation Classification
Two reference lists are created to classify Thanas into two analytical groups:

Inside City Corporation, representing Thanas within the City Corporation area.
Outside City Corporation, representing Thanas outside the City Corporation but still within the analysis boundary.

These lists are later used to split the dataset and calculate separate summaries for each group.

thanas_outside_cc = ["Mirsharai", "Fatikchhari", "Raozan", "Sitakunda", "Hathazari", "Boalkhali", "Patiya", "Chandanaish", "Sandwip", "Rangunia", "Anowara", "Satkania", "Banshkhali", "Lohagara"]
thanas_in_cc = ["Bayejid Bostami", "Chandgaon", "Bakalia", "Kotwali", "Double Mooring", "Patenga", "Chittagong Port", "Halishahar", "Pahartali", "Khulshi", "Panchlaish"]
crumbs: 4  Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis

title: 4  Thana-Level Population Distribution and Age Group Analysis
text:
4.7 Standardizing Thana Names
Some records contain alternative spellings, informal names, administrative variants, or localized labels for the same Thana. A mapping dictionary is used to standardize these names into the classification labels used in the analysis.
This step is important because inconsistent naming can split the same geographic area into multiple categories and distort count-based summaries.

mapping = {
    "Firingibazar": "Kotwali",
    "EPZ": "Chittagong Port",
    "Lalkhan Bazar": "Khulshi",
    "Patharghata": "Kotwali",
    "Kaptai": "Rangunia",
    "Fouzdarhat": "Sitakunda",
    "New Mooring": "Chittagong Port",
    "Agrabad": "Double Mooring",
    "Karnaphuli": "Patiya",
    "Bhujpur": "Fatikchhari",
    "Chawk Bazar": "Panchlaish",
    "Bandar": "Chittagong Port",
    "Anwara":"Anowara",
    "Sadarghat": "Patiya",
    "Akbar Shah":"Pahartali",
    "Bayazid Bostami":"Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bandarban Sadar":"Satkania"
}

The mapping is applied to the Thana column. Any value not found in the mapping dictionary is retained as its original value.

df["Thana"] = df["Thana"].map(mapping).fillna(df["Thana"])

4.12 Creating City Corporation and Outside City Corporation Subsets
The dataset is divided into two filtered DataFrames:

df_cc: records belonging to Thanas inside the City Corporation.
df_outside: records belonging to Thanas outside the City Corporation.

Creating these subsets simplifies downstream calculations and keeps the analysis more readable.

# Pre-filter DataFrames for cleaner execution
df_cc = df[df["Thana"].isin(thanas_in_cc)]
df_outside = df[df["Thana"].isin(thanas_outside_cc)]

The following grouped summaries are calculated:

Total City Corporation records by age group.
City Corporation Thana-level records by age group.
Outside City Corporation Thana-level records by age group.
Total outside City Corporation records by age group.

These grouped outputs support both aggregate and location-specific demographic interpretation.

# Total records in City Corporation grouped by Age Group
cc_records_by_age = df_cc.groupby("Age Group").size()

4.22 Interpretation Guide
Use the generated outputs as follows:

Total Population shows the number of valid records after filtering and removing missing Thana values.

Age Group summaries show whether records are concentrated in the <=30 or >30 group.
Within-group percentages explain internal composition, such as the age distribution inside a specific Thana.
Global percentages show how much each group contributes to the entire filtered dataset.
Verification check confirms whether all standardized Thana records are covered by either the City Corporation list or the outside City Corporation list.

title: 5  Spatial Distribution of Diabetes Patients and Age Group Composition in Chattogram City Corporation
text:
5.1 City Corporation
This section visualizes the distribution of diabetes patients across selected thanas of Chattogram City Corporation. The map uses GADM level-3 administrative boundary data from a GeoPackage file and joins it with manually entered thana-level diabetes percentage data.
The spatial boundary file path is loaded from the .env file using the GPKG_PATH environment variable. This keeps the source code cleaner and avoids hard-coding the data path directly inside the analysis script.
The workflow follows these main steps:
The diabetes percentages are classified into the following groups:
A custom red color palette is used for the choropleth map. Unlike very pale choropleth palettes, the first class begins with a visibly darker red shade so that even low-percentage areas remain clearly visible on the map.
Show Code
# ============================
# Load Required Libraries
# ============================

import os
import re

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from matplotlib.backends.backend_agg import FigureCanvasAgg as FigureCanvas
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
from IPython.display import display


# ============================
# Load Environment Variable
# ============================

load_dotenv()

GPKG_PATH = os.getenv("GPKG_PATH")

if GPKG_PATH is None:
    raise ValueError("GPKG_PATH not found in .env file.")

if not os.path.exists(GPKG_PATH):
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"GPKG file not found at: {GPKG_PATH}")


# ============================
# Create Output Directory
# ============================

REPORT_DIR = "report"

os.makedirs(REPORT_DIR, exist_ok=True)


# ============================
# Helper Functions
# ============================

def clean_key(x):
    """
    Standardize text for joining:
    lowercase + remove all non-letter characters.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    return re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", str(x).lower())


def strip_admin_suffix(x):
    """
    Remove common administrative suffixes from place names.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    pattern = r"\s+(Sadar Upazila|Sadar|Upazila|Thana|PS)$"

    return re.sub(pattern, "", str(x), flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()


# ============================
# Load Spatial Data
# ============================

bd_adm3 = gpd.read_file(GPKG_PATH)


# ============================
# Input Data: Diabetes Percentage
# ============================

diab_city = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "thana": [
            "Pahartali",
            "Halishahar",
            "Khulshi",
            "Chandgaon",
            "Kotwali",
            "Double Mooring",
            "Panchlaish",
            "Bayejid Bostami",
            "Bakalia",
            "Chittagong Port",
            "Patenga",
        ],
        "percent": [
            24.07,
            12.82,
            10.34,
            9.59,
            9.19,
            8.63,
            7.94,
            7.80,
            4.45,
            3.31,
            1.86,
        ],
    }
)


# ============================
# Manual Name Harmonization
# ============================

name_fixes = {
    "Pahartali": "Pahartali",
    "Halishahar": "Halishahar",
    "Khulshi": "Khulshi",
    "Chandgaon": "Chandgaon",
    "Kotwali": "Kotwali",
    "Double Mooring": "Double Mooring",
    "Panchlaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Panchaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Bayejid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayazid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Chittagong Port": "Chittagong Port",
    "Bakalia": "Bakalia",
    "Patenga": "Patenga",
}


# ============================
# Prepare Spatial Dataset
# ============================

bd_ctg = bd_adm3.loc[
    bd_adm3["NAME_2"].isin(["Chattogram", "Chittagong"])
].copy()

bd_ctg["thana_gadm"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"]
bd_ctg["name3_simple"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"].apply(strip_admin_suffix)
bd_ctg["name3_fixed"] = bd_ctg["name3_simple"].replace(name_fixes)
bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"] = bd_ctg["name3_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Diabetes Dataset
# ============================

diab_join = diab_city.copy()

diab_join["thana_fixed"] = diab_join["thana"].replace(name_fixes)
diab_join["join_key_diab"] = diab_join["thana_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Join Spatial and Diabetes Data
# ============================

map_dat = bd_ctg.merge(
    diab_join,
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_diab",
)


# ============================
# Join Quality Check
# ============================

missing_thanas = diab_join.loc[
    ~diab_join["join_key_diab"].isin(map_dat["join_key_gadm"])
].copy()

if len(missing_thanas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_thanas["thana"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched thanas detected: {unmatched}")


# ============================
# Classification Setup
# ============================

vals = map_dat["percent"].dropna()

if len(vals) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No percentage values available for binning.")

max_percent = vals.max()

breaks = [
    1,
    3,
    5,
    7,
    9,
    11,
    13,
    max_percent,
]

diabetes_labels = [
    "1–3%",
    "3–5%",
    "5–7%",
    "7–9%",
    "9–11%",
    "11–13%",
    ">13%",
]


# ============================
# Apply Classification
# ============================

map_dat["pct_bin"] = pd.cut(
    map_dat["percent"],
    bins=breaks,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

map_dat["label_name"] = map_dat["name3_fixed"]

map_dat_plot = map_dat.loc[
    map_dat["percent"].notna()
].copy()

map_dat_plot["pct_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    map_dat_plot["pct_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Diabetes Choropleth Palette
# ============================

red_band = [
    "#FCBBA1",
    "#FC9272",
    "#FB6A4A",
    "#EF3B2C",
    "#CB181D",
    "#A50F15",
    "#67000D",
]

diabetes_palette = sns.color_palette(
    red_band,
    n_colors=len(diabetes_labels),
).as_hex()

diabetes_color_map = dict(zip(diabetes_labels, diabetes_palette))

map_dat_plot["fill_color"] = map_dat_plot["pct_bin"].map(diabetes_color_map)

outline_col = "#737373"
outline_lwd = 0.75


# ============================
# Render Map
# ============================

fig = Figure(figsize=(12, 14))
canvas = FigureCanvas(fig)

ax = fig.subplots()

map_dat_plot.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=map_dat_plot["fill_color"].tolist(),
    edgecolor=outline_col,
    linewidth=outline_lwd,
    zorder=1,
)


# ============================
# Add Thana Labels
# ============================

for _, row in map_dat_plot.iterrows():
    point = row.geometry.representative_point()

    ax.text(
        point.x,
        point.y,
        row["label_name"],
        fontsize=8,
        color="#1A1A1A",
        ha="center",
        va="center",
        bbox={
            "facecolor": "white",
            "alpha": 0.75,
            "edgecolor": "none",
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.20",
        },
        zorder=5,
    )


# ============================
# Diabetes Legend
# ============================

diabetes_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=diabetes_color_map[label],
        edgecolor=outline_col,
        label=label,
    )
    for label in diabetes_labels
]

diabetes_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=diabetes_legend_handles,
    title="Diabetes\n Patients(%)",
    loc="upper right",
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)


# ============================
# Save PDF and Display Figure
# ============================

ax.set_title("")
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_aspect("equal")

diabetes_map_pdf = os.path.join(
    REPORT_DIR,
    "chattogram_city_diabetes_choropleth_map.pdf",
)

fig.savefig(
    diabetes_map_pdf,
    format="pdf",
    bbox_inches="tight",
    pad_inches=0.20,
)

display(fig)
Figure: Spatial distribution of diabetes patients across selected thanas of Chattogram City Corporation. The choropleth colors represent diabetes patient percentage classes, with darker red indicating higher percentages.

text:
5.2 City Corporation with Age Group Composition
This section extends the diabetes choropleth map by adding miniature pie charts on top of each thana. The base map still shows diabetes percentage classes, while the pie charts show the age-group distribution within each thana.
Each miniature pie chart represents two age groups:

<=30 years
>30 years

The map therefore combines two layers of information:

Background choropleth layer: diabetes patient percentage by thana.
Overlay pie-chart layer: age-group distribution by thana.

The pie charts are shifted slightly above each polygon’s own representative point, while labels remain exactly at the representative point. This prevents labels from drifting into neighboring polygons.

# ============================
# Load Required Libraries
# ============================

import os
import re
import numpy as np

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.patheffects as path_effects

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from matplotlib.backends.backend_agg import FigureCanvasAgg as FigureCanvas
from matplotlib.patches import Patch, Wedge, Circle
from IPython.display import display


# ============================
# Load Environment Variable
# ============================

load_dotenv()

GPKG_PATH = os.getenv("GPKG_PATH")

if GPKG_PATH is None:
    raise ValueError("GPKG_PATH not found in .env file.")

if not os.path.exists(GPKG_PATH):
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"GPKG file not found at: {GPKG_PATH}")


# ============================
# Create Output Directory
# ============================

REPORT_DIR = "report"

os.makedirs(REPORT_DIR, exist_ok=True)


# ============================
# Helper Functions
# ============================

def clean_key(x):
    """
    Standardize text for joining:
    lowercase + remove all non-letter characters.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    return re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", str(x).lower())


def strip_admin_suffix(x):
    """
    Remove common administrative suffixes from place names.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    pattern = r"\s+(Sadar Upazila|Sadar|Upazila|Thana|PS)$"

    return re.sub(pattern, "", str(x), flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()


def draw_pie_with_percentages(
    ax,
    x,
    y,
    values,
    colors,
    text_colors,
    radius,
    label_fontsize=6,
    edgecolor="white",
    linewidth=0.50,
):
    """
    Draw a miniature pie chart at a map coordinate
    and place percentage labels inside each slice.
    """
    total = sum(values)

    if total <= 0:
        return

    start_angle = 90

    for value, color, text_color in zip(values, colors, text_colors):
        angle = 360 * value / total
        end_angle = start_angle + angle

        wedge = Wedge(
            center=(x, y),
            r=radius,
            theta1=start_angle,
            theta2=end_angle,
            facecolor=color,
            edgecolor=edgecolor,
            linewidth=linewidth,
            zorder=6,
        )

        ax.add_patch(wedge)

        mid_angle = (start_angle + end_angle) / 2
        mid_angle_rad = np.deg2rad(mid_angle)

        label_x = x + radius * 0.58 * np.cos(mid_angle_rad)
        label_y = y + radius * 0.58 * np.sin(mid_angle_rad)

        label_text = f"{value:.1f}%"

        stroke_color = "white" if text_color == "black" else "black"

        ax.text(
            label_x,
            label_y,
            label_text,
            fontsize=label_fontsize,
            color=text_color,
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            weight="bold",
            zorder=9,
            path_effects=[
                path_effects.withStroke(
                    linewidth=1.2,
                    foreground=stroke_color,
                )
            ],
        )

        start_angle = end_angle

    outline = Circle(
        xy=(x, y),
        radius=radius,
        facecolor="none",
        edgecolor="#222222",
        linewidth=0.50,
        zorder=7,
    )

    ax.add_patch(outline)


# ============================
# Load Spatial Data
# ============================

bd_adm3 = gpd.read_file(GPKG_PATH)


# ============================
# Input Data: Diabetes Percentage
# ============================

diab_city = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "thana": [
            "Pahartali",
            "Halishahar",
            "Khulshi",
            "Chandgaon",
            "Kotwali",
            "Double Mooring",
            "Panchlaish",
            "Bayejid Bostami",
            "Bakalia",
            "Chittagong Port",
            "Patenga",
        ],
        "percent": [
            24.07,
            12.82,
            10.34,
            9.59,
            9.19,
            8.63,
            7.94,
            7.80,
            4.45,
            3.31,
            1.86,
        ],
    }
)


# ============================
# Input Data: Age Group Percentages
# ============================

age_city = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "thana": [
            "Bakalia",
            "Bayejid Bostami",
            "Chandgaon",
            "Chittagong Port",
            "Double Mooring",
            "Halishahar",
            "Khulshi",
            "Kotwali",
            "Pahartali",
            "Panchlaish",
            "Patenga",
        ],
        "age_le_30": [
            9.57,
            9.60,
            8.50,
            8.44,
            7.74,
            7.97,
            10.73,
            6.71,
            10.10,
            6.94,
            9.88,
        ],
        "age_gt_30": [
            90.43,
            90.40,
            91.50,
            91.56,
            92.26,
            92.03,
            89.27,
            93.29,
            89.90,
            93.06,
            90.12,
        ],
    }
)


# ============================
# Manual Name Harmonization
# ============================

name_fixes = {
    "Pahartali": "Pahartali",
    "Halishahar": "Halishahar",
    "Khulshi": "Khulshi",
    "Chandgaon": "Chandgaon",
    "Kotwali": "Kotwali",
    "Double Mooring": "Double Mooring",
    "Panchlaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Panchaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Bayejid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayazid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Chittagong Port": "Chittagong Port",
    "Bakalia": "Bakalia",
    "Patenga": "Patenga",
}


# ============================
# Prepare Spatial Dataset
# ============================

bd_ctg = bd_adm3.loc[
    bd_adm3["NAME_2"].isin(["Chattogram", "Chittagong"])
].copy()

bd_ctg["thana_gadm"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"]
bd_ctg["name3_simple"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"].apply(strip_admin_suffix)
bd_ctg["name3_fixed"] = bd_ctg["name3_simple"].replace(name_fixes)
bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"] = bd_ctg["name3_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Diabetes Dataset
# ============================

diab_join = diab_city.copy()

diab_join["thana_fixed"] = diab_join["thana"].replace(name_fixes)
diab_join["join_key_diab"] = diab_join["thana_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Age Group Dataset
# ============================

age_join = age_city.copy()

age_join["thana_fixed"] = age_join["thana"].replace(name_fixes)
age_join["join_key_age"] = age_join["thana_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Join Spatial, Diabetes, and Age Data
# ============================

map_dat = bd_ctg.merge(
    diab_join,
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_diab",
)

map_dat = map_dat.merge(
    age_join[["join_key_age", "age_le_30", "age_gt_30"]],
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_age",
)


# ============================
# Join Quality Checks
# ============================

missing_diabetes_thanas = diab_join.loc[
    ~diab_join["join_key_diab"].isin(map_dat["join_key_gadm"])
].copy()

if len(missing_diabetes_thanas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_diabetes_thanas["thana"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched diabetes thanas detected: {unmatched}")


missing_age_thanas = age_join.loc[
    ~age_join["join_key_age"].isin(map_dat["join_key_gadm"])
].copy()

if len(missing_age_thanas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_age_thanas["thana"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched age-group thanas detected: {unmatched}")


# ============================
# Classification Setup
# ============================

vals = map_dat["percent"].dropna()

if len(vals) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No percentage values available for binning.")

max_percent = vals.max()

breaks = [
    1,
    3,
    5,
    7,
    9,
    11,
    13,
    max_percent,
]

diabetes_labels = [
    "1–3%",
    "3–5%",
    "5–7%",
    "7–9%",
    "9–11%",
    "11–13%",
    ">13%",
]


# ============================
# Apply Diabetes Classification
# ============================

map_dat["pct_bin"] = pd.cut(
    map_dat["percent"],
    bins=breaks,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

map_dat["label_name"] = map_dat["name3_fixed"]

map_dat_plot = map_dat.loc[
    map_dat["percent"].notna()
].copy()

map_dat_plot["pct_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    map_dat_plot["pct_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Diabetes Choropleth Palette
# ============================

red_band = [
    "#FCBBA1",
    "#FC9272",
    "#FB6A4A",
    "#EF3B2C",
    "#CB181D",
    "#A50F15",
    "#67000D",
]

diabetes_palette = sns.color_palette(
    red_band,
    n_colors=len(diabetes_labels),
).as_hex()

diabetes_color_map = dict(zip(diabetes_labels, diabetes_palette))

map_dat_plot["fill_color"] = map_dat_plot["pct_bin"].map(diabetes_color_map)

outline_col = "#737373"
outline_lwd = 0.75


# ============================
# Age Group Pie Chart Palette
# ============================

age_labels = ["<=30", ">30"]

age_colors = sns.color_palette(
    ["#FFD166", "#118AB2"],
    n_colors=len(age_labels),
).as_hex()

age_color_map = dict(zip(age_labels, age_colors))

age_text_color_map = {
    "<=30": "black",
    ">30": "white",
}


# ============================
# Render Map
# ============================

fig = Figure(figsize=(12, 14))
canvas = FigureCanvas(fig)

ax = fig.subplots()

map_dat_plot.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=map_dat_plot["fill_color"].tolist(),
    edgecolor=outline_col,
    linewidth=outline_lwd,
    zorder=1,
)


# ============================
# Pie Chart Size
# ============================

minx, miny, maxx, maxy = map_dat_plot.total_bounds

map_width = maxx - minx
map_height = maxy - miny

pie_radius = min(map_width, map_height) * 0.040


# ============================
# Add Labels at True Representative Points
# ============================

label_points = {}

for _, row in map_dat_plot.iterrows():
    point = row.geometry.representative_point()
    label_points[row["label_name"]] = point

    ax.text(
        point.x,
        point.y,
        row["label_name"],
        fontsize=8,
        color="#1A1A1A",
        ha="center",
        va="center",
        bbox={
            "facecolor": "white",
            "alpha": 0.80,
            "edgecolor": "none",
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.20",
        },
        zorder=10,
    )


# ============================
# Draw Age Group Pie Charts Slightly Above Labels
# ============================

for _, row in map_dat_plot.iterrows():
    if pd.isna(row["age_le_30"]) or pd.isna(row["age_gt_30"]):
        continue

    point = label_points[row["label_name"]]

    pie_x = point.x
    pie_y = point.y + pie_radius * 1.35

    draw_pie_with_percentages(
        ax=ax,
        x=pie_x,
        y=pie_y,
        values=[
            row["age_le_30"],
            row["age_gt_30"],
        ],
        colors=[
            age_color_map["<=30"],
            age_color_map[">30"],
        ],
        text_colors=[
            age_text_color_map["<=30"],
            age_text_color_map[">30"],
        ],
        radius=pie_radius,
        label_fontsize=6,
    )


# ============================
# Diabetes Legend
# ============================

diabetes_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=diabetes_color_map[label],
        edgecolor=outline_col,
        label=label,
    )
    for label in diabetes_labels
]

diabetes_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=diabetes_legend_handles,
    title="Diabetes\n Patients(%)",
    loc="upper right",
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)

ax.add_artist(diabetes_legend)


# ============================
# Age Group Legend
# ============================

age_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=age_color_map["<=30"],
        edgecolor="#333333",
        label="<=30 years",
    ),
    Patch(
        facecolor=age_color_map[">30"],
        edgecolor="#333333",
        label=">30 years",
    ),
]

age_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=age_legend_handles,
    title="Age Group",
    loc=(0.6,0.9),
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

age_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
age_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)


# ============================
# Save PDF and Display Figure
# ============================

ax.set_title("")
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_aspect("equal")

age_group_map_pdf = os.path.join(
    REPORT_DIR,
    "chattogram_city_diabetes_age_group_pie_map.pdf",
)

fig.savefig(
    age_group_map_pdf,
    format="pdf",
    bbox_inches="tight",
    pad_inches=0.20,
)

display(fig)









Figure: Diabetes patient distribution and age-group composition across selected thanas of Chattogram City Corporation. The red choropleth background shows diabetes patient percentage classes, while miniature pie charts show the proportion of patients aged <=30 years and >30 years in each thana.

text:
6.1 Chattogram District and City Corporation
This section visualizes the spatial distribution of diabetes patient shares across Chattogram. The map combines two spatial units:
The Chittagong City Corporation area is created by merging its component thanas into a single polygon. This allows comparison between the overall City Corporation share and the outside-CC upazila shares.
The spatial boundary file path is loaded from the .env file using the GPKG_PATH environment variable.
The latest diabetes patient share data used in this map are:
The diabetes patient shares are classified into fixed classes:

# ============================
# Load Required Libraries
# ============================

import os
import re

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from matplotlib.backends.backend_agg import FigureCanvasAgg as FigureCanvas
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
from IPython.display import display


# ============================
# Load Environment Variable
# ============================

load_dotenv()

GPKG_PATH = os.getenv("GPKG_PATH")

if GPKG_PATH is None:
    raise ValueError("GPKG_PATH not found in .env file.")

if not os.path.exists(GPKG_PATH):
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"GPKG file not found at: {GPKG_PATH}")


# ============================
# Create Output Directory
# ============================

REPORT_DIR = "report"

os.makedirs(REPORT_DIR, exist_ok=True)


# ============================
# Helper Functions
# ============================

def clean_key(x):
    """
    Standardize text for joining:
    lowercase + remove all non-letter characters.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    return re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", str(x).lower())


def strip_admin_suffix(x):
    """
    Remove common administrative suffixes from place names.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    pattern = r"\s+(Sadar Upazila|Sadar|Upazila|Thana|PS)$"

    return re.sub(pattern, "", str(x), flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()


def union_geometries(geometry_series):
    """
    Safely union geometries across GeoPandas/Shapely versions.
    """
    if hasattr(geometry_series, "union_all"):
        return geometry_series.union_all()

    return geometry_series.unary_union


# ============================
# Load Spatial Data
# ============================

bd_adm3 = gpd.read_file(GPKG_PATH)


# ============================
# Input Data: Diabetes Share
# ============================

diab_outside_cc = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "upazila": [
            "Sitakunda",
            "Patiya",
            "Mirsharai",
            "Raozan",
            "Rangunia",
            "Sandwip",
            "Fatikchhari",
            "Hathazari",
            "Banshkhali",
            "Boalkhali",
            "Anowara",
            "Satkania",
            "Chandanaish",
            "Lohagara",
        ],
        "percent": [
            11.03,
            7.71,
            5.70,
            5.10,
            4.89,
            4.88,
            4.80,
            4.30,
            4.26,
            3.99,
            3.93,
            3.39,
            2.34,
            1.79,
        ],
    }
)

ccc_percent = 31.89


# ============================
# Name Harmonization
# ============================

name_fixes = {
    "Anwara": "Anowara",
    "Anowara": "Anowara",
    "Mirsarai": "Mirsharai",
    "Mirsharai": "Mirsharai",
    "Fatikchari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Fatickchari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Fatikchhari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Sitakunda": "Sitakunda",
    "Sitakund": "Sitakunda",
}


# ============================
# City Corporation Thana Names
# ============================

ccc_thanas_raw = [
    "Pahartali",
    "Patenga",
    "Chittagong Port",
    "Double Mooring",
    "Halishahar",
    "Khulshi",
    "Panchaish",
    "Panchlaish",
    "Kotwali",
    "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid Bostami",
    "Bayezid",
    "Bayazid",
    "Chandgaon",
    "Bakalia",
]

ccc_keys = [
    clean_key(strip_admin_suffix(name))
    for name in ccc_thanas_raw
]


# ============================
# Prepare Chattogram Spatial Dataset
# ============================

bd_ctg = bd_adm3.loc[
    bd_adm3["NAME_2"].isin(["Chattogram", "Chittagong"])
].copy()

bd_ctg["upazila_gadm"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"]
bd_ctg["name3_simple"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"].apply(strip_admin_suffix)
bd_ctg["name3_fixed"] = bd_ctg["name3_simple"].replace(name_fixes)
bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"] = bd_ctg["name3_fixed"].apply(clean_key)
bd_ctg["is_ccc_thana"] = bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"].isin(ccc_keys)


# ============================
# Prepare Diabetes Dataset
# ============================

diab_join = diab_outside_cc.copy()

diab_join["upazila_fixed"] = diab_join["upazila"].replace(name_fixes)
diab_join["join_key_diab"] = diab_join["upazila_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Join Spatial and Diabetes Data
# ============================

map_dat = bd_ctg.merge(
    diab_join,
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_diab",
)

map_dat["label_name"] = map_dat["name3_fixed"]


# ============================
# Join Quality Check
# ============================

map_join_keys = set(map_dat["join_key_gadm"].dropna())

missing_upazilas = diab_join.loc[
    ~diab_join["join_key_diab"].isin(map_join_keys)
].copy()

if len(missing_upazilas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_upazilas["upazila"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched outside-CC upazilas detected: {unmatched}")


# ============================
# Classification Setup
# ============================

available_percent = map_dat["percent"].dropna()

if len(available_percent) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No percentage values available for binning.")

max_percent = max(
    available_percent.max(),
    ccc_percent,
)

breaks_viz = [
    1,
    3,
    5,
    7,
    9,
    11,
    13,
    max_percent,
]

diabetes_labels = [
    "1–3%",
    "3–5%",
    "5–7%",
    "7–9%",
    "9–11%",
    "11–13%",
    ">13%",
]


# ============================
# Prepare Non-CCC Upazilas
# ============================

map_non_ccc = map_dat.loc[
    (~map_dat["is_ccc_thana"]) & (map_dat["percent"].notna())
].copy()

map_non_ccc["viz_bin"] = pd.cut(
    map_non_ccc["percent"],
    bins=breaks_viz,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

map_non_ccc["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    map_non_ccc["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Merge CCC Thanas into One Polygon
# ============================

ccc_parts = bd_ctg.loc[
    bd_ctg["is_ccc_thana"]
].copy()

if len(ccc_parts) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No City Corporation thana polygons were found.")

ccc_union_geometry = union_geometries(ccc_parts.geometry)

ccc_union = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "label_name": ["Chittagong City Corporation"],
        "percent": [ccc_percent],
        "geometry": [ccc_union_geometry],
    },
    crs=bd_ctg.crs,
)

ccc_union["viz_bin"] = pd.cut(
    ccc_union["percent"],
    bins=breaks_viz,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

ccc_union["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    ccc_union["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Combine Non-CCC and CCC
# ============================

schema_cols = [
    "label_name",
    "percent",
    "viz_bin",
    "geometry",
]

plot_all = pd.concat(
    [
        map_non_ccc[schema_cols],
        ccc_union[schema_cols],
    ],
    ignore_index=True,
)

plot_all = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    plot_all,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs=bd_ctg.crs,
)

plot_all = plot_all.loc[
    plot_all["viz_bin"].notna()
].copy()

plot_all["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    plot_all["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Diabetes Choropleth Palette
# ============================

red_band = [
    "#FCBBA1",
    "#FC9272",
    "#FB6A4A",
    "#EF3B2C",
    "#CB181D",
    "#A50F15",
    "#67000D",
]

diabetes_palette = sns.color_palette(
    red_band,
    n_colors=len(diabetes_labels),
).as_hex()

diabetes_color_map = dict(zip(diabetes_labels, diabetes_palette))

plot_all["fill_color"] = plot_all["viz_bin"].map(diabetes_color_map)

outline_col = "#737373"
outline_lwd = 0.75


# ============================
# Render Map
# ============================

fig = Figure(figsize=(12, 14))
canvas = FigureCanvas(fig)

ax = fig.subplots()

plot_all.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=plot_all["fill_color"].tolist(),
    edgecolor=outline_col,
    linewidth=outline_lwd,
    zorder=1,
)


# ============================
# Add Labels
# ============================

for _, row in plot_all.iterrows():
    point = row.geometry.representative_point()

    label_text = row["label_name"]
    label_fontsize = 8

    if row["label_name"] == "Chittagong City Corporation":
        label_text = "Chittagong City\nCorporation"

    ax.text(
        point.x,
        point.y,
        label_text,
        fontsize=label_fontsize,
        color="#1A1A1A",
        ha="center",
        va="center",
        bbox={
            "facecolor": "white",
            "alpha": 0.75,
            "edgecolor": "none",
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.20",
        },
        zorder=5,
    )


# ============================
# Diabetes Legend
# ============================

diabetes_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=diabetes_color_map[label],
        edgecolor=outline_col,
        label=label,
    )
    for label in diabetes_labels
]

diabetes_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=diabetes_legend_handles,
    title="Diabetes Patients\n(%)",
    loc="upper right",
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)


# ============================
# Save PDF and Display Figure
# ============================

ax.set_title("")
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_aspect("equal")

district_map_pdf = os.path.join(
    REPORT_DIR,
    "chattogram_diabetes_share_map.pdf",
)

fig.savefig(
    district_map_pdf,
    format="pdf",
    bbox_inches="tight",
    pad_inches=0.20,
)

display(fig)

text:
6.2 Chattogram District and City Corporation with Age Group Composition
This section extends the previous choropleth map by adding miniature pie charts showing age-group composition. Each pie chart represents the percentage of diabetes patients in two age groups:

<=30 years
>30 years

For outside-CC upazilas, each pie chart uses the corresponding upazila-level age-group breakdown. For the merged City Corporation polygon, the pie chart uses the overall City Corporation age-group breakdown:

<=30 years: 8.85%
>30 years: 91.15%

In this map, labels are placed directly at each polygon’s own representative point. The pie charts are shifted slightly upward from the same representative point. This prevents labels from drifting into neighboring polygons.

# ============================
# Load Required Libraries
# ============================

import os
import re
import numpy as np

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.patheffects as path_effects

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from matplotlib.backends.backend_agg import FigureCanvasAgg as FigureCanvas
from matplotlib.patches import Patch, Wedge, Circle
from IPython.display import display


# ============================
# Load Environment Variable
# ============================

load_dotenv()

GPKG_PATH = os.getenv("GPKG_PATH")

if GPKG_PATH is None:
    raise ValueError("GPKG_PATH not found in .env file.")

if not os.path.exists(GPKG_PATH):
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"GPKG file not found at: {GPKG_PATH}")


# ============================
# Create Output Directory
# ============================

REPORT_DIR = "report"

os.makedirs(REPORT_DIR, exist_ok=True)


# ============================
# Helper Functions
# ============================

def clean_key(x):
    """
    Standardize text for joining:
    lowercase + remove all non-letter characters.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    return re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", str(x).lower())


def strip_admin_suffix(x):
    """
    Remove common administrative suffixes from place names.
    """
    if pd.isna(x):
        return None

    pattern = r"\s+(Sadar Upazila|Sadar|Upazila|Thana|PS)$"

    return re.sub(pattern, "", str(x), flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()


def union_geometries(geometry_series):
    """
    Safely union geometries across GeoPandas/Shapely versions.
    """
    if hasattr(geometry_series, "union_all"):
        return geometry_series.union_all()

    return geometry_series.unary_union


def draw_pie_with_percentages(
    ax,
    x,
    y,
    values,
    colors,
    text_colors,
    radius,
    label_fontsize=6,
    edgecolor="white",
    linewidth=0.50,
):
    """
    Draw a miniature pie chart at a map coordinate
    and place percentage labels inside each slice.
    """
    total = sum(values)

    if total <= 0:
        return

    start_angle = 90

    for value, color, text_color in zip(values, colors, text_colors):
        angle = 360 * value / total
        end_angle = start_angle + angle

        wedge = Wedge(
            center=(x, y),
            r=radius,
            theta1=start_angle,
            theta2=end_angle,
            facecolor=color,
            edgecolor=edgecolor,
            linewidth=linewidth,
            zorder=6,
        )

        ax.add_patch(wedge)

        mid_angle = (start_angle + end_angle) / 2
        mid_angle_rad = np.deg2rad(mid_angle)

        label_x = x + radius * 0.58 * np.cos(mid_angle_rad)
        label_y = y + radius * 0.58 * np.sin(mid_angle_rad)

        label_text = f"{value:.1f}%"

        stroke_color = "white" if text_color == "black" else "black"

        ax.text(
            label_x,
            label_y,
            label_text,
            fontsize=label_fontsize,
            color=text_color,
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            weight="bold",
            zorder=9,
            path_effects=[
                path_effects.withStroke(
                    linewidth=1.2,
                    foreground=stroke_color,
                )
            ],
        )

        start_angle = end_angle

    outline = Circle(
        xy=(x, y),
        radius=radius,
        facecolor="none",
        edgecolor="#222222",
        linewidth=0.50,
        zorder=7,
    )

    ax.add_patch(outline)


# ============================
# Load Spatial Data
# ============================

bd_adm3 = gpd.read_file(GPKG_PATH)


# ============================
# Input Data: Diabetes Share
# ============================

diab_outside_cc = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "upazila": [
            "Sitakunda",
            "Patiya",
            "Mirsharai",
            "Raozan",
            "Rangunia",
            "Sandwip",
            "Fatikchhari",
            "Hathazari",
            "Banshkhali",
            "Boalkhali",
            "Anowara",
            "Satkania",
            "Chandanaish",
            "Lohagara",
        ],
        "percent": [
            11.03,
            7.71,
            5.70,
            5.10,
            4.89,
            4.88,
            4.80,
            4.30,
            4.26,
            3.99,
            3.93,
            3.39,
            2.34,
            1.79,
        ],
    }
)

ccc_percent = 31.89


# ============================
# Input Data: Age Group Percentages
# ============================

age_outside_cc = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "upazila": [
            "Anowara",
            "Banshkhali",
            "Boalkhali",
            "Chandanaish",
            "Fatikchhari",
            "Hathazari",
            "Lohagara",
            "Mirsharai",
            "Patiya",
            "Rangunia",
            "Raozan",
            "Sandwip",
            "Satkania",
            "Sitakunda",
        ],
        "age_le_30": [
            8.41,
            9.52,
            7.57,
            7.91,
            7.42,
            7.90,
            7.19,
            7.11,
            8.54,
            7.77,
            6.75,
            6.39,
            9.06,
            10.15,
        ],
        "age_gt_30": [
            91.59,
            90.48,
            92.43,
            92.09,
            92.58,
            92.10,
            92.81,
            92.89,
            91.46,
            92.23,
            93.25,
            93.61,
            90.94,
            89.85,
        ],
    }
)

ccc_age_le_30 = 8.85
ccc_age_gt_30 = 91.15


# ============================
# Name Harmonization
# ============================

name_fixes = {
    "Anwara": "Anowara",
    "Anowara": "Anowara",
    "Mirsarai": "Mirsharai",
    "Mirsharai": "Mirsharai",
    "Fatikchari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Fatickchari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Fatikchhari": "Fatikchhari",
    "Sitakunda": "Sitakunda",
    "Sitakund": "Sitakunda",
}


# ============================
# City Corporation Thana Names
# ============================

ccc_thanas_raw = [
    "Pahartali",
    "Patenga",
    "Chittagong Port",
    "Double Mooring",
    "Halishahar",
    "Khulshi",
    "Panchaish",
    "Panchlaish",
    "Kotwali",
    "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid Bostami",
    "Bayezid",
    "Bayazid",
    "Chandgaon",
    "Bakalia",
]

ccc_keys = [
    clean_key(strip_admin_suffix(name))
    for name in ccc_thanas_raw
]


# ============================
# Prepare Chattogram Spatial Dataset
# ============================

bd_ctg = bd_adm3.loc[
    bd_adm3["NAME_2"].isin(["Chattogram", "Chittagong"])
].copy()

bd_ctg["upazila_gadm"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"]
bd_ctg["name3_simple"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"].apply(strip_admin_suffix)
bd_ctg["name3_fixed"] = bd_ctg["name3_simple"].replace(name_fixes)
bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"] = bd_ctg["name3_fixed"].apply(clean_key)
bd_ctg["is_ccc_thana"] = bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"].isin(ccc_keys)


# ============================
# Prepare Diabetes Dataset
# ============================

diab_join = diab_outside_cc.copy()

diab_join["upazila_fixed"] = diab_join["upazila"].replace(name_fixes)
diab_join["join_key_diab"] = diab_join["upazila_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Age Group Dataset
# ============================

age_join = age_outside_cc.copy()

age_join["upazila_fixed"] = age_join["upazila"].replace(name_fixes)
age_join["join_key_age"] = age_join["upazila_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Join Spatial, Diabetes, and Age Data
# ============================

map_dat = bd_ctg.merge(
    diab_join,
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_diab",
)

map_dat = map_dat.merge(
    age_join[["join_key_age", "age_le_30", "age_gt_30"]],
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_age",
)

map_dat["label_name"] = map_dat["name3_fixed"]


# ============================
# Join Quality Checks
# ============================

map_join_keys = set(map_dat["join_key_gadm"].dropna())

missing_upazilas = diab_join.loc[
    ~diab_join["join_key_diab"].isin(map_join_keys)
].copy()

if len(missing_upazilas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_upazilas["upazila"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched outside-CC upazilas detected: {unmatched}")


missing_age_upazilas = age_join.loc[
    ~age_join["join_key_age"].isin(map_join_keys)
].copy()

if len(missing_age_upazilas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_age_upazilas["upazila"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched outside-CC age-group upazilas detected: {unmatched}")


# ============================
# Classification Setup
# ============================

available_percent = map_dat["percent"].dropna()

if len(available_percent) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No percentage values available for binning.")

max_percent = max(
    available_percent.max(),
    ccc_percent,
)

breaks_viz = [
    1,
    3,
    5,
    7,
    9,
    11,
    13,
    max_percent,
]

diabetes_labels = [
    "1–3%",
    "3–5%",
    "5–7%",
    "7–9%",
    "9–11%",
    "11–13%",
    ">13%",
]


# ============================
# Prepare Non-CCC Upazilas
# ============================

map_non_ccc = map_dat.loc[
    (~map_dat["is_ccc_thana"]) & (map_dat["percent"].notna())
].copy()

map_non_ccc["viz_bin"] = pd.cut(
    map_non_ccc["percent"],
    bins=breaks_viz,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

map_non_ccc["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    map_non_ccc["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Merge CCC Thanas into One Polygon
# ============================

ccc_parts = bd_ctg.loc[
    bd_ctg["is_ccc_thana"]
].copy()

if len(ccc_parts) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No City Corporation thana polygons were found.")

ccc_union_geometry = union_geometries(ccc_parts.geometry)

ccc_union = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "label_name": ["Chittagong City Corporation"],
        "percent": [ccc_percent],
        "age_le_30": [ccc_age_le_30],
        "age_gt_30": [ccc_age_gt_30],
        "geometry": [ccc_union_geometry],
    },
    crs=bd_ctg.crs,
)

ccc_union["viz_bin"] = pd.cut(
    ccc_union["percent"],
    bins=breaks_viz,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

ccc_union["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    ccc_union["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Combine Non-CCC and CCC
# ============================

schema_cols = [
    "label_name",
    "percent",
    "age_le_30",
    "age_gt_30",
    "viz_bin",
    "geometry",
]

plot_all = pd.concat(
    [
        map_non_ccc[schema_cols],
        ccc_union[schema_cols],
    ],
    ignore_index=True,
)

plot_all = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    plot_all,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs=bd_ctg.crs,
)

plot_all = plot_all.loc[
    plot_all["viz_bin"].notna()
].copy()

plot_all["viz_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    plot_all["viz_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Diabetes Choropleth Palette
# ============================

red_band = [
    "#FCBBA1",
    "#FC9272",
    "#FB6A4A",
    "#EF3B2C",
    "#CB181D",
    "#A50F15",
    "#67000D",
]

diabetes_palette = sns.color_palette(
    red_band,
    n_colors=len(diabetes_labels),
).as_hex()

diabetes_color_map = dict(zip(diabetes_labels, diabetes_palette))

plot_all["fill_color"] = plot_all["viz_bin"].map(diabetes_color_map)

outline_col = "#737373"
outline_lwd = 0.75


# ============================
# Age Group Pie Chart Palette
# ============================

age_labels = ["<=30", ">30"]

age_colors = sns.color_palette(
    ["#FFD166", "#118AB2"],
    n_colors=len(age_labels),
).as_hex()

age_color_map = dict(zip(age_labels, age_colors))

age_text_color_map = {
    "<=30": "black",
    ">30": "white",
}


# ============================
# Render Map
# ============================

fig = Figure(figsize=(12, 14))
canvas = FigureCanvas(fig)

ax = fig.subplots()

plot_all.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=plot_all["fill_color"].tolist(),
    edgecolor=outline_col,
    linewidth=outline_lwd,
    zorder=1,
)


# ============================
# Pie Chart Size
# ============================

minx, miny, maxx, maxy = plot_all.total_bounds

map_width = maxx - minx
map_height = maxy - miny

pie_radius = min(map_width, map_height) * 0.040


# ============================
# Add Labels at True Representative Points
# ============================

label_points = {}

for _, row in plot_all.iterrows():
    point = row.geometry.representative_point()
    label_points[row["label_name"]] = point

    label_text = row["label_name"]
    label_fontsize = 8

    if row["label_name"] == "Chittagong City Corporation":
        label_text = "Chittagong City\nCorporation"

    ax.text(
        point.x,
        point.y,
        label_text,
        fontsize=label_fontsize,
        color="#1A1A1A",
        ha="center",
        va="center",
        bbox={
            "facecolor": "white",
            "alpha": 0.80,
            "edgecolor": "none",
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.20",
        },
        zorder=10,
    )


# ============================
# Draw Age Group Pie Charts Slightly Above Labels
# ============================

for _, row in plot_all.iterrows():
    if pd.isna(row["age_le_30"]) or pd.isna(row["age_gt_30"]):
        continue

    point = label_points[row["label_name"]]

    pie_x = point.x
    pie_y = point.y + pie_radius * 1.35

    draw_pie_with_percentages(
        ax=ax,
        x=pie_x,
        y=pie_y,
        values=[
            row["age_le_30"],
            row["age_gt_30"],
        ],
        colors=[
            age_color_map["<=30"],
            age_color_map[">30"],
        ],
        text_colors=[
            age_text_color_map["<=30"],
            age_text_color_map[">30"],
        ],
        radius=pie_radius,
        label_fontsize=6,
    )


# ============================
# Diabetes Legend
# ============================

diabetes_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=diabetes_color_map[label],
        edgecolor=outline_col,
        label=label,
    )
    for label in diabetes_labels
]

diabetes_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=diabetes_legend_handles,
    title="Diabetes Patients\n(%)",
    loc="upper right",
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)

ax.add_artist(diabetes_legend)


# ============================
# Age Group Legend
# ============================

age_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=age_color_map["<=30"],
        edgecolor="#333333",
        label="<=30 years",
    ),
    Patch(
        facecolor=age_color_map[">30"],
        edgecolor="#333333",
        label=">30 years",
    ),
]

age_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=age_legend_handles,
    title="Age Group",
    loc=(0.7,0.9),
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=8,
    title_fontsize=9,
)

age_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
age_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)


# ============================
# Save PDF and Display Figure
# ============================

ax.set_title("")
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_aspect("equal")

district_age_map_pdf = os.path.join(
    REPORT_DIR,
    "chattogram_diabetes_share_age_group_map.pdf",
)

fig.savefig(
    district_age_map_pdf,
    format="pdf",
    bbox_inches="tight",
    pad_inches=0.20,
)

display(fig)









Figure: Diabetes patient share and age-group composition across outside-CC upazilas and the merged Chittagong City Corporation area. The red choropleth background shows diabetes patient share classes, while miniature pie charts show the proportion of patients aged <=30 years and >30 years.

text:
7.1 City Corporation Diabetes Share and Control Status
This section visualizes diabetes patient share across selected thanas of Chattogram City Corporation using percentages calculated against the total City Corporation population.
The choropleth background shows the percentage share of diabetes patients by thana. Miniature pie charts are placed on top of each thana to show the distribution of fasting control status:
The spatial boundary file path is loaded from the .env file using the GPKG_PATH environment variable.

    for value, color, text_color in zip(values, colors, text_colors):
        angle = 360 * value / total
        end_angle = start_angle + angle

        wedge = Wedge(
            center=(x, y),
            r=radius,
            theta1=start_angle,
            theta2=end_angle,
            facecolor=color,
            edgecolor=edgecolor,
            linewidth=linewidth,
            zorder=6,
        )

        ax.add_patch(wedge)

        mid_angle = (start_angle + end_angle) / 2
        mid_angle_rad = np.deg2rad(mid_angle)

        label_x = x + radius * 0.58 * np.cos(mid_angle_rad)
        label_y = y + radius * 0.58 * np.sin(mid_angle_rad)

        label_text = f"{value:.1f}%"

        stroke_color = "white" if text_color == "black" else "black"

        ax.text(
            label_x,
            label_y,
            label_text,
            fontsize=label_fontsize,
            color=text_color,
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            weight="bold",
            zorder=9,
            path_effects=[
                path_effects.withStroke(
                    linewidth=1.25,
                    foreground=stroke_color,
                )
            ],
        )

        start_angle = end_angle

    outline = Circle(
        xy=(x, y),
        radius=radius,
        facecolor="none",
        edgecolor="#222222",
        linewidth=0.55,
        zorder=7,
    )

    ax.add_patch(outline)


# ============================
# Load Spatial Data
# ============================

bd_adm3 = gpd.read_file(GPKG_PATH)


# ============================
# Input Data: Diabetes Share by Thana
# Based on Total City Corporation Population
# ============================

diab_city = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "thana": [
            "Bakalia",
            "Bayejid Bostami",
            "Chandgaon",
            "Chittagong Port",
            "Double Mooring",
            "Halishahar",
            "Khulshi",
            "Kotwali",
            "Pahartali",
            "Panchlaish",
            "Patenga",
        ],
        "percent": [
            0.2680,
            0.2708,
            0.4183,
            0.1316,
            0.4712,
            0.7443,
            0.4961,
            0.3697,
            1.2322,
            0.3209,
            0.1014,
        ],
    }
)


# ============================
# Input Data: Control Status Distribution by Thana
# ============================

control_city = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "thana": [
            "Bakalia",
            "Bayejid Bostami",
            "Chandgaon",
            "Chittagong Port",
            "Double Mooring",
            "Halishahar",
            "Khulshi",
            "Kotwali",
            "Pahartali",
            "Panchlaish",
            "Patenga",
        ],
        "controlled": [
            16.67,
            18.55,
            13.09,
            12.00,
            14.81,
            15.93,
            18.12,
            15.75,
            17.03,
            16.10,
            18.18,
        ],
        "not_controlled": [
            83.33,
            81.45,
            86.91,
            88.00,
            85.19,
            84.07,
            81.88,
            84.25,
            82.97,
            83.90,
            81.82,
        ],
    }
)


# ============================
# Manual Name Harmonization
# ============================

name_fixes = {
    "Bakalia": "Bakalia",
    "Bayejid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayezid Bostami": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Bayazid": "Bayejid Bostami",
    "Chandgaon": "Chandgaon",
    "Chittagong Port": "Chittagong Port",
    "Double Mooring": "Double Mooring",
    "Halishahar": "Halishahar",
    "Khulshi": "Khulshi",
    "Kotwali": "Kotwali",
    "Pahartali": "Pahartali",
    "Panchlaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Panchaish": "Panchlaish",
    "Patenga": "Patenga",
}


# ============================
# Prepare Spatial Dataset
# ============================

bd_ctg = bd_adm3.loc[
    bd_adm3["NAME_2"].isin(["Chattogram", "Chittagong"])
].copy()

bd_ctg["thana_gadm"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"]
bd_ctg["name3_simple"] = bd_ctg["NAME_3"].apply(strip_admin_suffix)
bd_ctg["name3_fixed"] = bd_ctg["name3_simple"].replace(name_fixes)
bd_ctg["join_key_gadm"] = bd_ctg["name3_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Diabetes Dataset
# ============================

diab_join = diab_city.copy()

diab_join["thana_fixed"] = diab_join["thana"].replace(name_fixes)
diab_join["join_key_diab"] = diab_join["thana_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Prepare Control Status Dataset
# ============================

control_join = control_city.copy()

control_join["thana_fixed"] = control_join["thana"].replace(name_fixes)
control_join["join_key_control"] = control_join["thana_fixed"].apply(clean_key)


# ============================
# Join Spatial, Diabetes, and Control Data
# ============================

map_dat = bd_ctg.merge(
    diab_join,
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_diab",
)

map_dat = map_dat.merge(
    control_join[["join_key_control", "controlled", "not_controlled"]],
    how="left",
    left_on="join_key_gadm",
    right_on="join_key_control",
)


# ============================
# Join Quality Checks
# ============================

missing_diabetes_thanas = diab_join.loc[
    ~diab_join["join_key_diab"].isin(map_dat["join_key_gadm"])
].copy()

if len(missing_diabetes_thanas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_diabetes_thanas["thana"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched diabetes thanas detected: {unmatched}")


missing_control_thanas = control_join.loc[
    ~control_join["join_key_control"].isin(map_dat["join_key_gadm"])
].copy()

if len(missing_control_thanas) > 0:
    unmatched = ", ".join(missing_control_thanas["thana"].unique())
    raise ValueError(f"Unmatched control-status thanas detected: {unmatched}")


# ============================
# Classification Setup
# ============================

vals = map_dat["percent"].dropna()

if len(vals) == 0:
    raise ValueError("No percentage values available for binning.")

max_percent = vals.max()

breaks = [
    0.10,
    0.25,
    0.40,
    0.55,
    0.70,
    0.85,
    1.00,
    max_percent,
]

diabetes_labels = [
    "0.10–0.25%",
    "0.25–0.40%",
    "0.40–0.55%",
    "0.55–0.70%",
    "0.70–0.85%",
    "0.85–1.00%",
    ">1.00%",
]


# ============================
# Apply Classification
# ============================

map_dat["pct_bin"] = pd.cut(
    map_dat["percent"],
    bins=breaks,
    labels=diabetes_labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True,
)

map_dat["label_name"] = map_dat["name3_fixed"]

map_dat_plot = map_dat.loc[
    map_dat["percent"].notna()
].copy()

map_dat_plot["pct_bin"] = pd.Categorical(
    map_dat_plot["pct_bin"],
    categories=diabetes_labels,
    ordered=True,
)


# ============================
# Diabetes Choropleth Palette
# ============================

red_band = [
    "#FCBBA1",
    "#FC9272",
    "#FB6A4A",
    "#EF3B2C",
    "#CB181D",
    "#A50F15",
    "#67000D",
]

diabetes_palette = sns.color_palette(
    red_band,
    n_colors=len(diabetes_labels),
).as_hex()

diabetes_color_map = dict(zip(diabetes_labels, diabetes_palette))

map_dat_plot["fill_color"] = map_dat_plot["pct_bin"].map(diabetes_color_map)

outline_col = "#737373"
outline_lwd = 0.75


# ============================
# Control Status Pie Chart Palette
# ============================

# Controlled: yellow
# Uncontrolled: bluish

control_labels = [
    "Controlled",
    "Uncontrolled",
]

control_colors = sns.color_palette(
    [
        "#F0E442",
        "#0072B2",
    ],
    n_colors=len(control_labels),
).as_hex()

control_color_map = dict(zip(control_labels, control_colors))

control_text_color_map = {
    "Controlled": "black",
    "Uncontrolled": "white",
}


# ============================
# Render Map
# ============================

fig = Figure(figsize=(12, 14))
canvas = FigureCanvas(fig)

ax = fig.subplots()

map_dat_plot.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=map_dat_plot["fill_color"].tolist(),
    edgecolor=outline_col,
    linewidth=outline_lwd,
    zorder=1,
)


# ============================
# Pie Chart Size
# ============================

minx, miny, maxx, maxy = map_dat_plot.total_bounds

map_width = maxx - minx
map_height = maxy - miny

pie_radius = min(map_width, map_height) * 0.040


# ============================
# Add Labels at True Representative Points
# ============================

label_points = {}

for _, row in map_dat_plot.iterrows():
    point = row.geometry.representative_point()
    label_points[row["label_name"]] = point

    ax.text(
        point.x,
        point.y,
        row["label_name"],
        fontsize=8,
        color="#1A1A1A",
        ha="center",
        va="center",
        bbox={
            "facecolor": "white",
            "alpha": 0.80,
            "edgecolor": "none",
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.20",
        },
        zorder=10,
    )


# ============================
# Draw Control Status Pie Charts Slightly Above Labels
# ============================

for _, row in map_dat_plot.iterrows():
    if pd.isna(row["controlled"]) or pd.isna(row["not_controlled"]):
        continue

    point = label_points[row["label_name"]]

    pie_x = point.x
    pie_y = point.y + pie_radius * 1.35

    draw_pie_with_percentages(
        ax=ax,
        x=pie_x,
        y=pie_y,
        values=[
            row["controlled"],
            row["not_controlled"],
        ],
        colors=[
            control_color_map["Controlled"],
            control_color_map["Uncontrolled"],
        ],
        text_colors=[
            control_text_color_map["Controlled"],
            control_text_color_map["Uncontrolled"],
        ],
        radius=pie_radius,
        label_fontsize=6,
    )


# ============================
# Diabetes Legend
# ============================

diabetes_legend_handles = [
    Patch(
        facecolor=diabetes_color_map[label],
        edgecolor=outline_col,
        label=label,
    )
    for label in diabetes_labels
]

diabetes_legend = ax.legend(
    handles=diabetes_legend_handles,

diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
diabetes_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)

control_legend = ax.legend(

control_legend.get_frame().set_facecolor("white")
control_legend.get_frame().set_alpha(0.95)

# Save PDF and Display Figure
# ============================

ax.set_title("")
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_aspect("equal")

control_status_map_pdf = os.path.join(

fig.savefig(
